title: Adapt a theory-led survey and mediation design to generative-AI use and academic engagement

research_logic: Use theory to connect an exposure, a mediator, and an outcome.
Estimate direct and indirect associations with alternative specifications.
Bound conclusions to the cross-sectional design.
strengths: The construct sequence makes the research question testable.
The mediation model gives each analysis a clear argumentative function.
Sensitivity models expose dependence on measurement and covariate choices.
context_bound_elements: The original exposure variable is narrower than generative-AI use.
The original population and validated scales may not transfer.
Cross-sectional timing cannot establish causal mediation.

user_project.topic: How generative-AI use relates to university students' academic engagement through academic self-efficacy.
available_materials: Access to university students for a one-time survey.
constraints: Limited ability to recruit across institutions.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Use one exposure variable in a theory-led mediation model.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Define distinct dimensions of generative-AI use before selecting an exposure measure.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Reuse validated measures for mediator and outcome.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Verify construct validity and reliability for the new population and language.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Interpret an indirect effect as a mechanism.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Frame the estimate as an indirect association unless temporal or causal evidence is added.

required_claim_boundaries: The study may report an indirect association pattern.
Generalization is limited to the sampled student context.
forbidden_inferences: Academic self-efficacy is a proven causal mechanism.
The sample represents all university students.
